data.title: Apple's FY25 EPS forecast to fall 28% unless given tariff exemption: Needham
data.description:
Apple's (NASDAQ:AAPL) full-year, fiscal 2025 earnings per share estimate is calculated to fall by 28%, or $2 per share, if the iPhone maker isn't granted an exemption from U.S. President Donald Trump's "Liberation Day" tariff plan, according to Needham.

Needham currently estimates a full-year fiscal 2025 EPS estimate of $7.32. If Apple is not granted an exemption, Needham expects it to fall to $5.27.

"EPS down 28% is NOT the worst case, which would include China retaliating against the US by banning all or some AAPL product sales in China, which were 17% of AAPL's total sales in FY24A," said Needham analyst Laura Martin and Dan Medina, in a Friday investor note.

In its analysis, Needham assumes the same estimated number of units are sold, and Apple absorbs the cost increases as consumers buy at the current retail prices. It also assumes that Apple products sold in the US, but manufactured in China, are subject to a 54% tariff. Products manufactured in India are subject to a 26% tariff. The tariffs are not expected to affect Apple's Services revenue.

Apple shares declined 9% by Thursday's close, causing its market capitalization to plunge nearly $300B. Shares were down another 4% during pre-market trading on Friday.

"By implication, Wall Street is betting there is a 30% chance AAPL will get an exemption because: Trump gave AAPL an exemption in 2018; on Feb 5, AAPL committed to spending $500B in the US over 4 years, creating 20,000 new jobs in engineering, R&D, manufacturing and AI; and, AAPL is the most iconic US brand in the world (our view)," Martin noted.

Apple releases its second quarter fiscal 2025 financial results on May 1. A consensus estimate expects Apple to report earnings per share of $1.61 on revenue of $94.04B.

data.title: European Stocks poised for earnings surprises in April
data.description:
Morgan Stanley analysts highlighted European stocks with the highest Earnings Surprise Composite scores.

These are stocks the quantitative equity research team believes will deliver positive earnings in the upcoming season, leveraging signals from their earnings forecast landscape, earnings quality, and broader forecast dynamics to deliver a composite score, Ronald Ho, quantitative strategist at Morgan Stanley.

Since 2019, this strategy has achieved a pre-cost Sharpe ratio of 0.68 for the selection of European stocks. Also, since 2024, the live performance of this strategy has resulted in a pre-cost Sharpe ratio of 1.89 for Europe.

The following are European stocks (NYSEARCA:IEUR) that are expected to report earnings between April 4 and May 2, that have an “overweight,” and that fall within the top two quintiles of the Earnings Surprise Composite scores.

  1. Barclays Bank (BCS) – Earnings surprise composite score: 1.00
  2. Adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY) – Earnings surprise composite score: 0.97

data.title: Jobs data in charts: Payroll growth expands, unemployment rate ticks up
Chain restaurants: Seeking Alpha Analysts vs. Wall Street
data.description:
Major chain restaurants are bracing for the disruption that the newly announced tariffs would cause their businesses. 

The industry's biggest concern pertains to the hike in food and packaging costs along with the uncertainty of sourcing ingredients to support customer demand. 

In this environment, Wall Street analysts have appeared to be more favorable toward these stocks than Seeking Alpha analysts. Chipotle Mexican Grill (NYSE:CMG) carried the highest number of Strong Buy recommendations. 

The Cheesecake Factory (NASDAQ:CAKE), Wendy's (NASDAQ:WEN) and Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI), which owns Olive Garden and LongHorn Steakhouse featured a wide range of opinions. 

SA analysts and the sell-side saw opinions match on Restaurant Brands International (NYSE:QSR), which owns chains like Burger King and Tim Horton’s, and Yum China Holdings (NYSE:YUMC) and Yum! (NYSE:YUM), which owns KFC, Pizza Hut, and Taco Bell. 

Conversely, McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) and Domino's Pizza (NASDAQ:DPZ) got a bullish tilt from Wall Street, while SA analysts remained cautious.

data.title: Arqit Quantum reports prelim revenue for H1
data.description:
Arqit Quantum (NASDAQ:ARQQ) expects preliminary revenues of around $18,000 to $67,000 for the first half of fiscal year 2025.

Cash and cash equivalents are estimated to be around $24.7M as of March 31, 2025. The company plans to report its complete first half results in May 2025.

Arqit was awarded a multi-year enterprise license contract in the EMEA region for a government end user, which was signed prior to the end of the 2024 fiscal year. Revenue generation was expected to commence early in the first half of fiscal year 2025.

However, due to end customer delay, revenue generation did not commence until late March, adversely affecting revenue for the period.

ARQQ --5.3% premarket to $12.5.

Source: Press Release

data.title: 'Nothing kills boat season like a market correction' - Baird
data.description:
As trade policy upends trading patterns and the correction expected to impact sentiment, Baird moved to the sidelines on its coverage of powersports and recreational vehicles to “protect dry powder for when the fog clears.”

“Tariffs threaten to upend the powersports ecosystem, rekindling retaliatory tariffs and an affordability crisis – potentially forcing uncomfortable structural change on survivors,” Baird analyst Craig Kennison said.

And there is nothing like an April market correction to kill a boat season, he adds. Kennison moves to Neutral from Overweight on Brunswick Corp (NYSE:BC), Malibu Boats (NASDAQ:MBUU), MasterCraft Boat Holdings (NASDAQ:MCFT), OneWater Marine (NASDAQ:ONEW), and Polaris (NYSE:PII), as tariff costs add to an already expensive market.

Although most RVs are made in America, parts are sourced globally, and Canada has imposed a 25% tariff on American-made RVs. While dealers have managed their inventory more effectively, weaker demand will drive shipments lower. For this reason, Kennison also downgraded Camping World Holdings (NYSE:CWH), LCI Industries (NYSE:LCII), Thor Industries (NYSE:THO), and Winnebago Industries (NYSE:WGO) to Neutral from Overweight.

He maintains his Overweight rating on names that offer limited direct tariff exposure, healthier balance sheets, and durable business models.

Among those are CarMax (NYSE:KMX) and OPENLANE (NYSE:KAR). While there is a risk in higher used car prices, the segment offers an alternative to the near certainty of higher new car prices.

The same is true for Copart (NASDAQ:CPRT) and RB Global (NYSE:RBA) which operate marketplaces for salvage vehicles.

“Policy uncertainty could fuel consignor hesitancy (limiting auction volume), but fees tied to auction prices will provide an inflation hedge,” says Kennison.

data.title: Essex Property Trust acquires $345.5M, disposes $366.6M worth of property YTD
data.description:
• Multi-family residential REIT Essex Property Trust (NYSE:ESS) announced on Thursday property acquisitions worth $345.5M and dispositions worth $366.6M year-to-date.
  • Shares were trading -3.06% Friday pre-market at $285.35.
  • "The transaction activity year to date results in a reallocation towards the Northern California region with the purchase of newer communities in submarkets where Essex expects lower supply and higher rent growth over the foreseeable future," the company said in a statement.
  • "The match funded transactions are net neutral to the company's 2025 core FFO forecast," said the statement.

data.title: Stock futures sink, yields tumble as China hits back with tariffs on U.S.
data.description:
The gloomy mood on Wall Street is expected to persist, with stock index futures plunging on Friday as China slapped a 34% tariff on U.S., intensifying fears of a U.S. recession and global trade war. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -2.9%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -2.9%, and Dow futures (INDU) -2.7%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 15 basis points to 3.90%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 17 basis points to 3.55%.

Two days after U.S. President Donald Trump announced sweeping tariffs against several countries, China has retaliated with 34% tariffs on all U.S. imports from April 10, reported by the country’s official Xinhua News Agency.

Nonfarm payrolls surprised to the upside in March, while unemployment edged up.

U.S. stocks were mauled Thursday, with the S&P 500 closing in a correction as President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs stoked fears of a global trade war. The S&P 500 closed at -4.8%, finishing its worst session since June 2020.

The selloff occurred after Trump late Wednesday detailed new tariffs that the U.S. is enacting on imports. The minimum baseline tariff will be 10%.

"The last 24 hours have been truly historic for markets, as the impact of the U.S. reciprocal tariffs cascaded across different asset classes, with no sign of letting up overnight," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"In terms of what happens now, the big question is how the U.S.’s trading partners might retaliate, as that will play a huge role in determining what the overall economic and market impact will be… On the back of all this, investors grew increasingly fearful about a potential US recession, with U.S. equities seeing their sharpest decline in years," Allen added.

Financial markets expected a significant tax increase from Trump. Yesterday’s reaction shows the tax increase was worse than anticipated. U.S. dollar weakness is telling. We often hear that when the U.S. sneezes the global economy catches cold. This is not the US sneezing. This is the U.S. cutting off its own arm, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"For now, US recession risks have risen. Over the longer term, effective tariff rates should fall somewhat. Rerouting supply chains, shifting demand patterns, and the political and economic pressure on Trump to retreat should reverse some of this damage," Donovan added. 

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is expected to speak at the SABEW conference in Arlington, Virginia.

data.title: Canada's unemployment rate rises to 6.7% in March
data.description:
The unemployment rate in Canada reported at 6.7% in March, matching estimates and higher than the previous month's 6.6%.

The labor force participation rate in Canada decreased to 65.20% in March from 65.30% in February.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA).

Currency: (USD:CAD) (CAD:USD)
